dialogos.texto_en: This plate looks nice too.\nI wonder if this pattern is some kind of leaf?
Oh, it's nice.\nThe color is vivid, and I think it's a good product.
Hey, are you two shopping together?
Oh, Uesugi-san, did you come over here too?
Yeah, is there something interesting%p-2;――
Fu-kun!
GUAAH?!
N-Nino?!
Hey, since we've run into each other and all that,\nlet's look around together, okay?
I said, don't suddenly stick to me like that.\nIt's dangerous, isn't it?
Awwww?\nBut if I don't,\nyou'll end up going somewhere else, right?
U-um, Nino?
What?
Well... Uesugi-san seems to be having a hard time,\nand, I mean...like, you know?
Oh, I see.
This is part of the memory making process.\nIf you're close friends, this is normal.
I-I see, that's right!\nThis is normal, right?
That's right, it's normal.\nYou're overthinking things, Yotsuba.
(Am I thinking too much too?\n Am I becoming too self-conscious again%p-2;――%p;)
(No, Yotsuba is here, I'm dating her...)
Nino, wait a minute%p-2;――
Hey, Fu-kun! Here, come here!
H-hey! Don't pull me like that all of a sudden!
There are a lot of pottery shops around here.\nThere are various dishes lined up,\nand it's fun just to look at them.
This is the first time I've ever gone around\nlooking at dishes.
So, do you like anything?
Hey, Uesugi-san! What is this 'yachimun'?
Ah, that's a word in the Okinawan dialect\nthat means pottery.
Oh, I see.\nYou know so much.
There are also pottery studios along the street,\nso if you look for them,\nyou can try pottery making, right?
Wow, that sounds interesting.
Hmmm...
Mmm? What's the matter?
It's nothing. Anyway, let's look at the plates.\nI wanted to get some new ones.
Oh, right. Did you find some you like?
Hmm, actually, there are too many good things,\nand I'm having trouble making a decision.
Maybe something that looks like it's Okinawa-ish\nwould be good. Like this blue one that's kinda sea-ish,\nor this one that's all tropical beach-ish.
What's with all the 'ish'?
Ehehe.\nSorry I can't express myself very well.
But, when choosing what to eat,\nthat kind of feeling is also important, you know?
That feeling? What do you mean?
Cooking isn't just about taste, it's also about savoring\nthe dish with your eyes. Even the same dish can give\na different impression depending on what it's served in.
Even if you don't notice it,\nwe're both bringing out each other's charms.
Well, I can see what you're trying to say...
Hey, Fu-kun?
HUH?!
I can bring out your charm, you know, Fu-kun?\nIn exchange, Fu-kun, bring out mine%p-2;――
N-Nino!\nHold on!
What, Yotsuba.
(What's going on?\n Nino suddenly got so aggressive...\n It can't be just my imagination, though, can it?)
This!\nHow about this dish? I think it suits your taste, Nino.
Ah! How nice!
There are various types of these dishes, aren't there?\nSeems you can collect them in a set\nfrom large plates to small ones.
You don't need those, right?\nHow many were you planning on buying?
Well, if you want to match your mood to the dish,\nyou can never have too many.
Nino really pays attention to her dishes.\nBut, I do feel like my appetite increases\nwhen the tableware matches the food.
If you choose that many of them, won't it be difficult\nto take them home? Even if you leave them at the hotel,\nyou have to be careful when transporting fragile items.
Oh, yeah. That's true.\nIt would be sad if we bought them\nand they broke on the way back.
You're too rough with your luggage.
Either way, there are delivery services, right?\nWe can just have them delivered\nto our apartment.
Oh, right.\nWe don't have to carry them ourselves.
This is Okinawa, you know?\nThe shipping costs would be ridiculous.
Well, compared to our travel expenses this time,\nit's still under budget for a normal trip.
(Grr... Is this the difference\n in the worlds we live in?!)
If you find something you like,\nhow about getting a whole set in the future?
Huh?
In the future?!
Oh my GOODNESS, Fu-kun.\nYou're already thinking that far ahead.
It's not that strange.\nYou have to think ahead and be prepared.
U-Uesugi-san?
Your dream is to open your own place, right, Nino?\nIt's not a bad idea to prepare for it now\nby thinking ahead.
Ah...\nThat's what you meant. I see.
*sigh*
What? What's wrong?
It's nothing.
But, you remembered that much, Fu-kun...\nThat's quite considerate of you, right?
Well okay, I think this is about it for now.\nWe just need to have them delivered to us.
Oh, you bought fewer than I expected.\nConsidering how enthusiastic you were earlier,\nI thought you were going to buy out everything.
I really want to, but if I get any more tableware,\nI'll start having trouble finding places to put it.
Huhh...\nJust how many dishes do you have...?
